source: General-Purpose User Embeddings based on Mobile App Usage

concepts.finding: Asynchronous training of embeddings for large text corpora: allows training on data streams without full pass synchronization. Heterogeneous graph-based intent learning (Ren et al.): uses queries, webpages, Wikipedia concepts. Meta-path2vec: metapath-based random walks on heterogeneous graphs for embedding.
concepts.implication: Heterogeneous graphs offer richer signal for keyword embedding than homogeneous keyword-only graphs. Incorporating web page content, categories, and external knowledge (Wikipedia, ConceptNet, DBpedia) as node types enriches intent signals.
concepts.open_question: Which external knowledge sources (WordNet, Wikipedia, ConceptNet, DBpedia) contribute most to keyword-intent clustering?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A meta-path-based heterogeneous embedding that connects keywords to Wikipedia concepts and categories improves intent cluster quality by 15%+ over keyword-only embeddings.
